rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRANCESCA P. NAIFY LIVING TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 30, 1990
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Christina Corteses Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Respondent John Sherwoods Demurrer As Untimely
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner Christina Cortese's Motion to Strike Demurrer as Untimely is denied. The language of the relevant statute is permissive rather than mandatory, and the Court may consider a demurrer filed more than thirty days after service of the complaint. McAllister v. Cty. of Monterey (2007) 147 Cal. App. 4th 253, 280. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, August 2, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRANCESCA P. NAIFY LIVING TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 30, 1990
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Re First Amended Petition For Relief
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is posted late. Respondent John Sherwood's Motion to Strike, in his individual capacity, is granted as to footnotes 2, 3, 5, and paragraphs 65, 66, 90, 91, 92, and 97 at lines 11 through 13 beginning with the case citation on line 11, of the First Amended Petition for Relief for Breach of Fiduciary Duty; Third-Party Liability for Participation in Breach of Trust; and Return of Trust Property. The Motion is otherwise denied. Petitione ris to file an amended petition in compliance with this order within ten (10) days of this hearing date. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, August 2, 2017.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FRANCESCA P. NAIFY LIVING TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 30, 1990
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of General Demurrer And General Demurrer To Second Cause Of Action Of First Amended Petition For Relief
rulings.Rulings: Respondent John Sherwood's General Demurrer to Second Cause of Action of First Amended Petition for Relief, in his individual capacity, is overruled. The Second Cause of Action does not facially bring an action under Civil Code § 1714.10, and it facially pleads facts sufficient for an equitable estoppel or tolling claim. Respondent is to file a response within ten (10) days of Petitioner filing an amended petition in compliance with the order granting Respondent's Motion to Strike heard concurrently with this Demurrer. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, August 2, 2017.
